summary.intent: Address a second round of Codex review findings on PR #694 for ars-ui, fixing RatingGroup aria/accessibility issues and TagGroup selection state bugs, then verify CI and coverage pass before retriggering Codex.
summary.outcome: All four Codex findings were patched (conditional aria-labelledby, disabled hidden input propagation, no-op rating suppression, TagGroup tombstone reconciliation), coverage passed at 98.5% line / 90.1% branch, and a clean commit was pushed with Codex retriggered.

repo: RatingGroup uses a hidden input that must mirror the group's disabled state — this was not wired up before this session.
TagGroup tracks removed keys as tombstones and these must be reconciled on prop sync to avoid stale selection state.
aria-labelledby on RatingGroup should only be emitted when an explicit label prop is present; unconditional emission causes a dangling reference.
The repo uses cargo-insta for snapshot testing; a single `cargo insta accept` run is needed after intentional output changes before tests pass.

The CI pipeline runs in stages: formatting → snapshot check → clippy → workspace check → unit tests → mutual exclusion → broader matrix (integration, DOM browser, error variants, coverage, feature flags).
Coverage threshold is enforced by `cargo xtask ci coverage` and merges native + wasm browser reports; the ars-components gate is 90% branch coverage.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment; threads are resolved via GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation.
The repo uses a skill workflow at `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` for polling Codex review state.

code.finding: control_attrs and hidden_input_attrs are the key functions for aria attribute generation; aria-labelledby must be gated on an optional label prop.
Rate/IncrementRating events that resolve to the same value as current state should return no transition/effect to suppress no-op commits.
Hidden input disabled attribute must be set when the RatingGroup is disabled.
SyncProps event handler must reconcile removed-key tombstones when items prop changes, otherwise previously removed keys persist in selection state.

workflow: Polling Codex review state requires querying both PR reactions (for in-progress signal) and unresolved review threads via GitHub GraphQL API.
The commit co-author trailer for Codex reviews is `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>`.
After pushing a fix commit, the `snapshot-reviewed` label must be re-applied with `gh pr edit --add-label snapshot-reviewed`.
Resolving all prior Codex threads before requesting a new `@codex review` ensures the next pass only surfaces new findings.
Running targeted `cargo test -p ars-components --lib data_display::rating_group::tests` before full coverage saves significant time catching syntax/logic errors early.
Test-local name shadowing (e.g., reusing `service` binding inside a test that already declares it) causes compile errors that are easy to miss when adding new test assertions inline.

friction: A test-local name shadowing compile error (`service` binding reused inside an existing test) required a fix-and-recompile cycle after the initial patch.
Coverage run is very long (full native + wasm browser matrix) with no partial progress visible until the final threshold table; this blocks the push for an extended period.
Codex review polling requires sleeping in 60–120s increments with no push notification, leading to many idle polling cycles.
The hidden-input disabled wiring was a repeat finding across two Codex passes, suggesting the first fix (92aadc3d) did not fully address it.
open_items: Verify that the next Codex pass on the new commit finds no further issues — the session ends before Codex completes its review of the follow-up commit.
The `rate_plan` function and `whole_mode_attrs` coverage was checked but not explicitly confirmed above the gate individually — worth monitoring if branch coverage dips on future changes.
The spec for RatingGroup was updated to document the label prop, but it is worth confirming the adapter layer surfaces this prop correctly in all supported framework adapters.
